# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Workflow

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set PR title and description to commit message
      run: echo "## [${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}] ${github.event.pull_request.body}" > COMMIT_MSG

    - name: Commit changes
      run: git add . && git commit -F COMMIT_MSG

    - name: Push changes
      uses: ad-m/github-push-action@v0.6.0
      with:
        github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        branch: main

    - name: Send notification
      if: github.event.pull_request.state == 'open'
      run: echo "PR ${github.event.pull_request.number}: ${github.event.pull_request.title} is open" | xargs -I {} slack-cli message post --channel #general --text {}
